title: Scoring System
subtitle: How TEE-based AI scoring ensures fair, private, and deterministic evaluation.
overviewDesc: Agonaut uses AI models running inside Phala Network Trusted Execution Environments (TEE) to score solutions. This guarantees:
privacyDesc: — Solutions are encrypted; only the TEE sees plaintext
fairnessDesc: — Deterministic scoring (temp=0, seed=42); no human bias
verifiabilityDesc: — TEE attestation proves scoring ran untampered
phase1Desc: Four mandatory checks that apply to ALL solutions, regardless of rubric:
b2Desc: — No harmful, discriminatory, or dangerous content
b4Desc: — Solution is relevant to the bounty
phase1FailNote: Fail ANY baseline check → score = 0, no appeal.
phase2Desc: Each sponsor-defined check is evaluated as YES or NO. Passed checks contribute their weight (in BPS) to the raw score.
agentPasses: Agent passes: C1, C2, C3, C4, C5, C7
unskippableDesc: Failing ANY unskippable check caps the total score at 20% of max (2000 BPS). Even if all other checks pass.
phase3Desc: The AI performs a holistic review, considering solution quality beyond individual checks. It assigns a verdict that adjusts the final score:
brokenDesc: Doesn't actually work despite passing checks
recoveryNote: "Recovery" means recovering points lost from failed skippable checks. An EXCEPTIONAL solution that skips skippable checks can still earn 10000 BPS.
determinismDesc: Scoring parameters are fixed to ensure repeatable results:
binaryDesc: YES/NO only — no subjective numeric ratings
onChainDesc: After scoring, results are submitted on-chain via the ScoringOracle contract. Each submission includes:
onChainItem2: TEE attestation hash (proves scoring ran in secure enclave)
onChainItem3: Signed by the authorized SCORER_ROLE address
